text.headline: Application Security: A Guided History
text.text: Explore the evolution of application security through key historical events

text.headline: The Morris Worm: First Major Internet Security Incident
text.text: The Morris Worm became the first major malware to spread across the internet, exposing vulnerabilities and highlighting the need for better security practices.

text.headline: OWASP Top 10 First Published
text.text: The OWASP Top 10 begins shaping a shared language for web application security risks, accelerating education and prioritization across the industry.

text.headline: SQL Slammer Worm Demonstrates Rapid Propagation
text.text: SQL Slammer exploited a buffer overflow in Microsoft SQL Server, spreading to 75,000 hosts in 10 minutes and demonstrating the speed of modern malware.

text.headline: Heartbleed: A Critical OpenSSL Vulnerability
text.text: Heartbleed exposed a critical vulnerability in OpenSSL affecting millions of web servers, demonstrating the risks of widespread dependency on open-source security libraries.

text.headline: Log4Shell: Critical RCE in Log4j
text.text: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) revealed a critical remote code execution vulnerability in Apache Log4j, one of the most widely used Java logging libraries, affecting millions of applications.

text.headline: CircleCI Security Breach: Session Token Compromise
text.text: A malware attack on a CircleCI engineer's laptop led to the theft of a 2FA-backed session token, resulting in unauthorized access to production systems and customer secrets, affecting all CircleCI customers.

text.headline: 3CX Supply Chain Attack: Trojanized VoIP Software
text.text: Attackers compromised 3CX's build system to distribute trojanized versions of the 3CX Desktop App to customers worldwide, demonstrating the continuing threat of sophisticated supply chain attacks.

text.headline: MOVEit Transfer: Mass Data Breach via SQL Injection
text.text: CVE-2023-34362, a critical SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer, was exploited by the CL0P ransomware group to exfiltrate data from hundreds of organizations globally, affecting millions of individuals.

text.headline: Okta Support System Breach: Session Token Theft
text.text: Attackers compromised Okta's customer support system using stolen credentials, accessing session tokens and customer data for nearly all Okta customers, highlighting risks even in identity management platforms.

text.headline: XZ Utils Backdoor: Near-Miss Supply Chain Attack
text.text: A sophisticated backdoor was discovered in XZ Utils, a core Linux compression library, after a multi-year social engineering campaign to gain maintainer access. Early detection prevented a catastrophic supply chain attack.
